Students will explore key domains such as marketing, finance, leadership, operations, and strategy, gaining practical insights into the challenges businesses face and the strategies they use to succeed. The course culminates in a comprehensive company analysis assignment, where students will apply their learning to evaluate a real-world organization's performance and strategic position.

title: Applied Research in Practice
description: This course provides a hands-on approach to applied research in practice, emphasizing the practical application of research and analytical skills. Students will engage with real-world business challenges, learning to formulate research questions, gather and interpret data, and draw evidence-based conclusions. Through a structured projects, students will develop effective communication skills, presenting insights and recommendations to support strategic business decisions. Designed to strengthen critical thinking and problem-solving abilities, this course prepares students to approach complex business issues with confidence and clarity.

title: Seminar in International Marketing
description: Explores environmental/cultural approach to international marketing and important global marketing trends, including growth/expansion of the world's big emerging markets, rapid growth of middle income market segments, and steady creation of regional market groups. Case studies are used to develop global and strategic thinking in terms of the marketing 4Ps.

title: Seminar in International Management
description: This seminar analyzes strategies and strategic responses of individual firms operating internationally. The evolution of global industries, global competition, and global strategies is emphasized throughout. Special emphasis is placed on the cultural differences between countries and their implications for international management efforts.

title: Seminar in Macroeconomic Analysis in the Global Economy
description: This seminar builds upon basic macroeconomic principles, applying economic models/theory to the global economies. Global macroeconomics seeks to explain the nature of association among economic forces in markets around the world, such as the impact of the United States fiscal deficit on increased foreign borrowing and forces causing the economic slump in Japan.

title: Seminar in World Economic Systems
description: This seminar explores differences among economic institutions, policy, and performance in countries around the world and provides information on the methods used to make economic comparisons across countries, examining the relative success or failure of these various economic states. Topics of discussion will revolve around major developments in the world economy.

title: Seminar in Entrepreneurship and Corporate Innovation
description: The course provides exposure to advanced concepts in entrepreneurship with an emphasis on leading entrepreneurship and innovation efforts in organizational enterprise settings. Students are taught to use all functional areas of business to optimize options to expand firm revenue, finance firm growth, and insure long-term sustainable value creation, while avoiding the pitfalls of improvisation that are common in innovative endeavors.

title: Selected Topics in Business Law
description: This course builds on the material from Health Care Jurisprudence and from Health Care Contracting and Negotiations. Topics reviewed may include sales, negotiable instruments, the corporation qua corporation, debtor-creditor relations, bankruptcy, real property, and the governmental regulation of business. Case studies will be developed by students and analyzed.

title: Managerial Accounting
description: This course introduces managerial accounting concepts and practices with a focus on healthcare organizations. Students will learn how to analyze financial information to support organizational and strategic decision-making, budgeting, and performance management in hospitals, clinics and other healthcare settings. Emphasis is placed on cost behavior, cost allocation, and variance analysis as tools for improving efficiency and accountability. Through case studies and applied exercises, students will develop the ability to interpret financial data and align resource use with organizational goals.

title: Organizational Ethics
description: This course will review major ethical theories, principles, decision-making methods, and the relationship between ethics and leadership. Clinical ethics topics will be considered from an organizational perspective, and topics with a more definitive business focus will be addressed. Case studies will be developed, analyzed, and discussed

title: Principles of Macroeconomics
description: This course introduces students to the performance of market economies. It addresses market dynamics that affect organizations and management decision-making in order to enhance the ability of the student to understand the context, source, and potential solutions for various problems and opportunities routinely encountered in the practice of management.

title: International Business
description: The objective of this course is to familiarize students with the environment in which international companies operate. Students will be introduced to the special problems and complexities of operating in the global marketplace, evaluating how and why businesses should explore international opportunities and the country entry strategies to accomplish those goals.

title: Advanced Shoulder and Elbow Injury Management
description: This doctoral-level course advances expertise in the comprehensive evaluation and management of complex shoulder and elbow sports injuries through integrated application of advanced regional anatomy, biomechanics, diagnostic imaging, and evidence-based surgical and non-surgical treatment strategies. Emphasis is placed on instability, rotator cuff pathology, labral injury, throwing-related conditions, and overuse syndromes. Learners synthesize pathophysiology, kinetic chain mechanics, rehabilitation science, and contemporary literature to support advanced clinical decision-making, optimize return-to-sport and functional outcomes, and prepare for independent specialty orthopaedic practice.

title: Advanced Lower Extremity Sports Injury Management
description: This doctoral-level course advances in the comprehensive evaluation and management of complex lower extremity sports injuries through integrated application of advanced anatomy, biomechanics, diagnostic imaging, and evidence-based surgical and non-surgical treatment strategies. Emphasis is placed on synthesizing pathophysiology, rehabilitation science, and contemporary literature to support advanced clinical decision-making, optimize return-to-activity and functional outcomes, and prepare learners for independent specialty practice.

title: Advanced Management of Hand and Elbow Disorders
description: This course advances the orthopaedic physician assistant’s expertise in the comprehensive evaluation and management of hand and elbow pathology by integrating advanced anatomy, biomechanics, diagnostic imaging, and evidence-based surgical and non-surgical treatment strategies. Emphasis is placed on microsurgical principles, fracture and dislocation management, tendon and nerve injury, degenerative conditions, and elbow instability. Learners synthesize pathophysiology, rehabilitation science, and contemporary literature to support advanced clinical decision-making and independent specialty practice across outpatient, inpatient, and austere environments.

title: Advanced Management of Foot and Ankle Disorders
description: This doctoral-level course advances the orthopaedic physician assistant’s expertise in the comprehensive evaluation and management of complex foot and ankle pathology by integrating advanced regional anatomy, biomechanics, gait analysis, diagnostic imaging, and evidence-based surgical and non-surgical treatment strategies. Emphasis is placed on trauma, deformity correction, tendon and ligament pathology, arthritic conditions, diabetic foot disorders, cartilage lesions, and limb preservation principles. Learners synthesize pathophysiology, rehabilitation science, and contemporary literature to support advanced clinical decision-making, optimize functional outcomes, and prepare for independent specialty orthopaedic practice.

title: Advanced Critical Care Management
description: This doctoral-level course advances the orthopaedic physician assistant’s expertise in the comprehensive management of critically ill and perioperative patients within intensive care and trauma environments. Emphasis is placed on advanced cardiopulmonary physiology, hemodynamic monitoring, resuscitation principles, multisystem organ dysfunction, damage control orthopaedics, sepsis management, and perioperative optimization. Learners integrate diagnostic imaging, laboratory interpretation, critical care pharmacology, and evidence-based protocols to support advanced decision-making across inpatient and austere environments.

title: Advanced Wound Care and Burns
description: This doctoral-level course advances the orthopaedic physician assistant’s expertise in the comprehensive evaluation and management of complex wounds and soft-tissue pathology, with an optional advanced focus on burn care. Emphasis is placed on wound pathophysiology, limb preservation principles, vascular and diabetic wound management, infection control, reconstructive strategies, grafting techniques, and burn depth assessment. Learners integrate advanced anatomy, tissue-healing biology, diagnostic imaging, laboratory evaluation, and evidence-based surgical and non-surgical treatment strategies to support complex soft-tissue management across inpatient, outpatient, and austere environments.

title: Advanced Joint Arthroplasty
description: This doctoral-level course advances the orthopaedic physician assistant’s expertise in the comprehensive evaluation and management of advanced degenerative joint disease by integrating arthroplasty biomechanics, implant design principles, diagnostic imaging, perioperative optimization, and evidence-based surgical and non-surgical treatment strategies. Emphasis is placed on primary and revision joint arthroplasty of the hip, knee, and shoulder; implant fixation concepts; alignment strategies; infection prevention; complication management; and longitudinal surveillance. Learners synthesize biomechanical principles, perioperative medicine, and contemporary literature to support advanced clinical decision-making and specialty orthopaedic practice.

title: Advanced Musculoskeletal Oncology
description: This doctoral-level course advances the orthopaedic physician assistant’s expertise in the comprehensive evaluation and management of musculoskeletal oncology and complex orthopaedic infections through integrated application of advanced regional anatomy, tumor biology, diagnostic imaging, histopathology principles, and evidence-based surgical and non-surgical treatment strategies. Emphasis is placed on benign and malignant bone and soft tissue tumors, metastatic disease, pathologic fractures, limb preservation, oncologic margins, adjuvant therapies, and oncologic surveillance. Learners synthesize oncologic staging principles, multidisciplinary care coordination, and contemporary literature to support advanced clinical decision-making and specialty orthopaedic practice.

title: Advanced General Trauma
description: This doctoral-level course advances the orthopaedic physician assistant’s expertise in the comprehensive evaluation and management of multisystem general trauma across the continuum of care. Emphasis is placed on trauma systems, Advanced Trauma Life Support (ATLS) principles, damage control resuscitation, shock states, hemorrhage control, thoracic and abdominal trauma, traumatic brain injury, and multisystem organ dysfunction. Learners integrate advanced anatomy, trauma physiology, imaging interpretation, laboratory analysis, and evidence-based surgical and non-surgical treatment strategies to support life-saving interventions, perioperative stabilization, and functional readiness across inpatient and austere environments.

title: Advanced Orthopaedic Trauma: Self Study
description: This doctoral-level course advances the orthopaedic physician assistant’s expertise in the comprehensive evaluation and management of orthopaedic trauma across the continuum of care. Emphasis is placed on fracture biomechanics, soft-tissue injury, open fracture management, limb preservation, damage control orthopaedics, fixation strategies, and operative stabilization principles. Learners integrate advanced musculoskeletal anatomy, imaging interpretation, fracture classification systems, and evidence-based surgical and non-surgical treatment strategies to support limb-saving interventions, functional restoration, and operational readiness across inpatient and austere environments.

title: Advanced Hip Sports Injury Management
description: This doctoral-level course advances expertise in the comprehensive evaluation and management of complex hip sports pathology by integrating advanced hip anatomy, biomechanics, diagnostic imaging, and evidence-based surgical and non-surgical treatment strategies. Emphasis is placed on femoroacetabular impingement, labral pathology, instability, hip dysplasia, athletic overuse syndromes, and post-traumatic conditions. Learners synthesize pathophysiology, rehabilitation science, and contemporary literature to support advanced clinical decision-making, optimize return-to-sport and functional outcomes, and prepare for independent specialty practice.

title: Introduction to Orthopaedic Clinical Evaluation and Procedures
description: This course introduces advanced principles of orthopaedic evaluation, diagnosis, and treatment necessary for regular clinical application, applying evidence-based medicine to the management of orthopaedic patients, and focusing on extremity anatomy, biomechanical pathology, physical examination, diagnostic imaging, and rehabilitation methods.

title: Evidence Based Orthopaedic Care
description: This course introduces the application of evidence-based medicine to the management of orthopaedic complaints, focusing on assessing current peer-reviewed journal articles for sound research design and valid conclusions so as to apply lessons learned from the literature to individual patients and patient populations.

title: Orthopaedic Evaluation and Management of Spine Disorders
description: This course introduces advanced principles of orthopaedic evaluation, diagnosis, and treatment necessary for regular clinical application, applying evidence-based medicine to the management of orthopaedic spine complaints, and focusing on spine anatomy, biomechanical pathology, physical examination, diagnostic imaging, and classification of common select fractures of the spine.

title: Evaluation and Management of Neurologic Disorders
description: This course introduces advanced principles of orthopaedic evaluation, diagnosis and treatment necessary for regular clinical application, applying evidence-based medicine to the management of neurologic complaints, and focusing on spine and head anatomy, biomechanical pathology, physical examination, diagnostic imaging, laboratory studies, and classification of common select fractures of the head and spine.

title: Evaluation and Management of Pediatric Orthopaedic Disorders
description: This course introduces advanced principles of orthopaedic evaluation, diagnosis, and treatment necessary for regular clinical application, applying evidence-based medicine to the management of pediatric orthopaedic complaints, and focusing on general anatomy, biomechanical pathology, physical examination, diagnostic imaging, laboratory studies, congenital disorders, and classification of pediatric fractures.

title: Advanced Orthopaedic Clinical Evaluation and Procedures
description: This course furthers critical principles of orthopaedic evaluation, diagnosis, and treatment necessary for regular clinical application, applying evidence-based medicine to the management of orthopaedic patients, and focusing on extremity anatomy, biomechanical pathology, physical examination, diagnostic imaging, and rehabilitation methods.

title: Techniques for Medical Research Presentation
description: This course introduces various techniques for medical research presentation. It takes information obtained in developing a high-quality clinical research project, demographic and outcome measure data, and statistical analysis, and generates oral and written products for presentation at local and national venues.

title: Advanced Management of Spine Disorders
description: This doctoral-level course advances the orthopedic physician assistant’s expertise in the comprehensive evaluation and management of spinal pathology by integrating advanced spinal anatomy, biomechanics, neuroanatomy, diagnostic imaging, and evidence-based surgical and non-surgical treatment strategies. Emphasis is placed on degenerative cervical and lumbar disease, radiculopathy, myelopathy, spinal trauma, deformity, instability, and spine-related functional impairment. Learners synthesize neurologic assessment, rehabilitation principles, and contemporary literature to support advanced clinical decision-making, optimize functional and functional outcomes, and prepare for independent specialty orthopedic practice.

title: Advanced Management of Pediatric Orthopaedic Disorders
description: This doctoral-level course advances the orthopaedic physician assistant’s expertise in the comprehensive evaluation and management of pediatric musculoskeletal pathology by integrating growth and developmental anatomy, biomechanics, diagnostic imaging, and evidence-based surgical and non-surgical treatment strategies. Emphasis is placed on pediatric trauma, congenital and developmental disorders, neuromuscular conditions, spinal deformity, and physeal injury. Learners synthesize pediatric pathophysiology, growth modulation principles, rehabilitation science, and contemporary literature to support advanced clinical decision-making, optimize functional outcomes, and prepare for independent specialty orthopaedic practice.

title: Orthopaedic Trauma Rotation
description: This is a two-month clinical and surgical rotation on service with a fellowship-trained orthopaedic traumatologist and a team of surgical residents.

title: Introduction to Upper Extremity Sports Injury Management
description: This course introduces advanced principles of orthopaedic evaluation, diagnosis, and treatment necessary for regular clinical application, applying evidence-based medicine to the management of patient athletes, and focusing on upper extremity anatomy, biomechanical pathology, physical examination, diagnostic imaging, and rehabilitation methods.

title: Introduction to Lower Extremity Sports Injury Management
description: This course introduces advanced principles of orthopaedic evaluation, diagnosis, and treatment necessary for regular clinical application, applying evidence-based medicine to the management of patient athletes, and focusing on lower extremity anatomy, biomechanical pathology, physical examination, diagnostic imaging, and rehabilitation methods.

title: Evaluation and Management of Hand and Elbow Disorders
description: This course introduces advanced principles of orthopaedic evaluation, diagnosis, and treatment necessary for regular clinical application; applies evidence-based medicine to the management of orthopaedic upper extremity complaints; and focuses on microsurgery, upper extremity anatomy, biomechanical pathology, physical examination, diagnostic imaging, and classification of common select fractures and dislocations of the upper extremity.

title: Evaluation and Management of Foot and Ankle Disorders
description: This course introduces advanced principles of orthopaedic evaluation, diagnosis, and treatment necessary for regular clinical application, applying evidence-based medicine to the management of orthopaedic complaints, and focusing on lower extremity anatomy, biomechanical pathology, physical examination, diagnostic imaging, and classification of common select fractures and dislocations of the leg, ankle, and foot.

title: Evaluation and Management of Complex Wounds
description: This course introduces advanced principles of orthopaedic evaluation, diagnosis, and treatment necessary for regular clinical application, applying evidence-based medicine to the management of patients with complex wounds., and focusing on general anatomy, biochemistry, physical examination, diagnostic imaging, laboratory studies, and medical and surgical management of complex wounds.

title: Evaluation of Joint Arthritis and Trauma Managed with Joint Reconstruction
description: This course introduces advanced principles of orthopaedic evaluation, diagnosis, and treatment necessary for regular clinical application, applying evidence-based medicine to the management of orthopaedic complaints, and focusing on general anatomy, biomechanical pathology, physical examination, diagnostic imaging, classification of periarticular fractures, and arthritis.

title: Musculoskeletal Oncology Evaluation and Management
description: This course introduces advanced principles of orthopaedic evaluation, diagnosis, and treatment necessary for regular clinical application, applying evidence-based medicine to the management of musculoskeletal neoplasms and infections, and focusing on general anatomy, biomechanical pathology, physical examination, diagnostic imaging, laboratory studies, and radiographic classification of oncologic lesions and pathologic fractures.

title: Introduction to Evaluation and Management of Orthopaedic Trauma
description: This course introduces advanced principles of orthopaedic evaluation, diagnosis, and treatment necessary for regular clinical application, applying evidence-based medicine to the management of musculoskeletal trauma, and focusing on general anatomy, biomechanical pathology, physical examination, diagnostic imaging, laboratory studies, and radiographic classification of orthopaedic trauma.

title: Introduction to Evaluation and Management of General Trauma
description: This course introduces advanced principles of surgical evaluation, diagnosis, and treatment necessary for regular clinical application, applying evidence-based medicine to the management of multisystem trauma, and focusing on general anatomy, biomechanical pathology, physical examination, diagnostic imaging, laboratory studies, and radiographic classification of multisystem trauma.

title: Introduction to Critical Care Management
description: This course introduces advanced principles of orthopaedic evaluation, diagnosis, and treatment necessary for regular clinical application, applying evidence-based medicine to the management of intensive care unit patients, and focusing on general anatomy, biomechanical pathology, physical examination, diagnostic imaging, laboratory studies, and medical management of critical patients.

title: Advanced Sports Injury Management
description: This course further examines critical principles of orthopaedic evaluation, diagnosis, and treatment necessary for regular clinical application, applying evidence-based medicine to the management of patient athletes, and focusing on extremity anatomy, biomechanical pathology, physical examination, diagnostic imaging, and rehabilitation methods.

title: Advanced evaluation and management of orthopaedic trauma
description: This course introduces advanced principles of orthopaedic evaluation, diagnosis, and treatment necessary for regular clinical application, applying evidence-based medicine to the management of musculoskeletal trauma, and focusing on general anatomy, biomechanical pathology, physical examination, diagnostic imaging, laboratory studies, and radiographic classification of orthopaedic trauma.

title: Advanced Critical Care Management
description: This course introduces advanced principles of orthopaedic evaluation, diagnosis, and treatment necessary for regular clinical application, applying evidence-based medicine to the management of intensive care unit patients, and focusing on general anatomy, biomechanical pathology, physical examination, diagnostic imaging, laboratory studies, and medical management of critical patients.

title: Medical Research Design
description: This course introduces medical research design, focusing on generating a testable research question, developing a research design that addresses the question, and conducting a literature review that supports the study design.

title: Approaches to Medical Data Collection and Analysis
description: This course introduces approaches to medical data collection and analysis, focusing on executing a research protocol, collecting outcome measures data, and then participating in the analysis of the data.

title: Urgent Orthopaedic Evaluation
description: This course introduces advanced principles of orthopaedic evaluation, diagnosis, and treatment necessary for regular clinical application, applying evidence-based medicine to the management of musculoskeletal trauma, and focusing on extremity anatomy, biomechanical pathology, physical examination, diagnostic imaging, laboratory studies, and radiographic classification of orthopaedic trauma.

title: Advanced Joint Reconstruction
description: This course advances principles of orthopaedic evaluation, diagnosis, and treatment necessary for regular clinical application. It applies evidence-based medicine to the management of orthopaedic complaints. The course focuses on general anatomy, biomechanical pathology, physical examination, diagnostic imaging, classification of periarticular fractures, and arthritis.

title: Introduction to Orthopaedic Clinical Evaluation and Procedures
description: This course introduces advanced principles of orthopaedic evaluation, diagnosis, and treatment necessary for regular clinical application, applying evidence-based medicine to the management of orthopaedic patients, and focusing on extremity anatomy, biomechanical pathology, physical examination, diagnostic imaging, and rehabilitation methods.

title: Evidence Based Orthopaedic Care
description: This course introduces the application of evidence-based medicine to the management of orthopaedic complaints, focusing on assessing current peer-reviewed journal articles for sound research design and valid conclusions so as to apply lessons learned from the literature to individual patients and patient populations.

title: Orthopaedic Evaluation and Management of Spine Disorders
description: This course introduces advanced principles of orthopaedic evaluation, diagnosis, and treatment necessary for regular clinical application, applying evidence-based medicine to the management of orthopaedic spine complaints, and focusing on spine anatomy, biomechanical pathology, physical examination, diagnostic imaging, and classification of common select fractures of the spine.

title: Evaluation and Management of Pediatric Orthopaedic Disorders
description: This course introduces advanced principles of orthopaedic evaluation, diagnosis, and treatment necessary for regular clinical application, applying evidence-based medicine to the management of pediatric orthopaedic complaints, and focusing on general anatomy, biomechanical pathology, physical examination, diagnostic imaging, laboratory studies, congenital disorders, and classification of pediatric fractures.

title: Advanced Orthopaedic Clinical Evaluation and Procedures
description: This course furthers critical principles of orthopaedic evaluation, diagnosis, and treatment necessary for regular clinical application, applying evidence-based medicine to the management of orthopaedic patients, and focusing on extremity anatomy, biomechanical pathology, physical examination, diagnostic imaging, and rehabilitation methods.

title: Seminar in Macroeconomic Analysis in the Global Economy
description: This seminar builds upon basic macroeconomic principles, applying economic models/theory to the global economies. Global macroeconomics seeks to explain the nature of association among economic forces in markets around the world, such as the impact of the United States fiscal deficit on increased foreign borrowing and forces causing the economic slump in Japan.

title: Seminar in World Economic Systems
description: This seminar explores differences among economic institutions, policy, and performance in countries around the world and provides information on the methods used to make economic comparisons across countries, examining the relative success or failure of these various economic states. Topics of discussion will revolve around major developments in the world economy.

title: Principles of Macroeconomics
description: This course introduces students to the performance of market economies. It addresses market dynamics that affect organizations and management decision-making in order to enhance the ability of the student to understand the context, source, and potential solutions for various problems and opportunities routinely encountered in the practice of management.

title: Managerial Economics
description: Managerial Economics concerns the efficient management of resources to achieve organizational or enterprise goals by applying economic theory and methodological techniques in the practice of management. This course is focused on the application of economic models, estimation techniques, and fundamental analysis in contemporary private and public markets.

title: Introduction to Robotics
description: Analysis of robot manipulators, including forward and inverse kinematics, rigid-body rotation parameterizations, velocity kinematics, path planning, nonlinear dynamics, single and multi-variable control.

title: Introduction to Aeronautics
description: Introduces the applied science of atmospheric flight. The course teaches about airplanes and how they fly from a design and application perspective. Included are topics in fluid dynamics, airfoil and wing theory, aircraft performance, stability, and aircraft design.

title: Analysis and Design of Propulsion Systems
description: Introduction to compressible flow, including flows with simple area change, heat addition, friction, and shock waves. Analysis, parametric design, and performance of ramjets, turbojets, turbofans, and turboprops. Introduction to the operating principles of major engine components. Introduction to rockets.

title: Solar Energy
description: A first course in the principles of solar energy collection, conversion and storage. Topics include solar photovoltaic and thermal collectors, sun-earth geometry, ground and sky radiation models, and balance-of-system components including stratified tanks, pumps, and power inverters. Students will learn industry-standard TRNSYS energy modeling software.

title: Selection of Materials and Manufacturing Processes in Design
description: Systematic approach for selection of materials and manufacturing process in design that balances performance requirements with cost of materials and manufacturing. Material properties, manufacturing processes and types of materials. Advanced computer software and case studies are used to illustrate application of principles.

title: Special Topics in Mechanical Engineering
description: Study of advanced topics in mechanical engineering.  This course may be repeated once under a different topic.

title: Special Projects in Mechanical Engineering
description: Advanced topics and/or special project activities in Mechanical Engineering.

title: Non-Thesis Degree Completion
description: To fulfill requirements for non-thesis master's students who need to complete final degree requirements other than coursework during their last semester.  This may include such things as a comprehensive examination, oral examination, or foreign language requirement. Students are required to be registered during the semester they graduate.

title: Engineering Analysis
description: See ELC 5302 for course information.

title: Computer-Aided Engineering and Design
description: Design and analysis of engineering components and systems using interactive computer programs with inclusion of computer simulation.

title: Introduction to Finite Element Methods
description: Introductory course on the theory and techniques of finite element analysis for numerical solutions of partial differential equations beginning from energy concepts and foundational constitutive equations. Numerical implementations and solutions are demonstrated by user-created code using modern computer technologies.

title: Advanced Dynamics
description: An advanced study of the mechanical dynamics of systems involving multiple, interconnected rigid bodies. Topics include mathematical expressions of body kinematics, various methods to derive dynamic equations of motion,  three-dimensional inertial properties, and dynamic motion constraints.

title: Advanced Finite Element Methods
description: Advanced analysis of the finite element theory with emphasis on non-linear applications for thermal and fluidic applications. Course will formulate the finite element form from several classes of constitutive equations, discuss solution methods, and construct and implement algorithms for solving the finite element form.

title: Applied Computational Fluid Dynamics
description: This course will teach the students how to use CFD (Computation Fluid Dynamics) software and will cover a variety of fluid flow problems.  The course’s main emphasis will be getting practical results which will include how to simplify geometry so that it can be analyzed, as well as properly meshing the model being generated.  After the simulation has been solved, another major part of the learning process will be understanding how to ensure that the results are valid.

title: Introduction to Nondestructive Testing and Evaluation
description: This course will begin by introducing the context of NDE as well as a brief history, followed by an introduction of some typical failures for various material systems.  The course will then focus on individual testing techniques, including: visual testing, penetrant testing, magnetic particle testing, radiographic testing, ultrasonic testing, eddy current testing, thermal infrared testing, acoustic emission testing, and digital radiography.

title: Introduction to Optimum Design
description: This course presents various optimal design methods that are common in simulation-based engineering design. The focus is on the development and application of design optimization numerical methods for engineering problems. Topics include fundamentals in optimal design methods, algorithm development, software application, and may include topics in multidisciplinary design optimization and structural optimization as time allows.

title: Engineering Ceramics
description: This course introduces students to engineering ceramic materials, mainly focusing on structural ceramics. Topics include general characteristics of ceramics, ceramic preparation and characterization, powder processing and sintering, ceramic phase diagrams, mechanical behavior of ceramics, ultra-high temperature ceramics and ceramic matrix composites.

title: Mechanical Behavior of Materials
description: This course explores the fundamental principles governing the mechanical behavior of engineering materials. Topics include stress-strain relationships, deformation mechanisms, fracture, fatigue, creep, and the influence of microstructure on material performance. Emphasis is placed on linking material properties to design applications and processing methods, with practical examples from structural and manufacturing contexts.

title: Introduction to Combustion
description: Understand and apply fundamentals for 1) combustion of gas, solid and liquid fuels; 2) combustion equilibrium and calculation of equilibrium compositions and flame temperature; 3) characterization of flame types; 4) quenching, flammability, ignition, and stabilization of flames; 5) soot formation; and 6) detonations and deflagrations.

title: Gas Dynamics
description: This course focuses on compressible flows, where the Mach number is high enough such that the assumption of incompressibility is no longer valid. Compressible flows occur externally and/or internally in many applications, including supersonic/hypersonic jets, rockets, missiles, and more. Topics covered include supersonic flows, shock waves, shock tubes, expansion fans, and compressible flows with friction or heat transfer.

title: Composite Materials
description: Introduction to composite materials, micromechanics of fiber reinforced composites, lamina mechanics, mechanical behavior of composite laminates, hygrothermal effects, and failure models.

title: Thermal Systems Design
description: Design, analysis, and simulation of thermal energy systems such as pipe networks, HVAC systems, and steam power plants. Specification of energy system components such as pumps, pipes, control valves, and heat exchangers. Estimation and optimization of initial and operational costs.

title: Experimental Methods in Heat Transfer and Fluid Flow
description: Consideration of experimental methods including experiment planning and design, error and uncertainty analysis, temperature measurement (in fluids and solids), flow rate measurement, flow visualization, and advanced data analysis; selected experiments conducted.

title: Tribology
description: Topics include the nature of rough surfaces, contact mechanics between noncomformal and nominally-flat surfaces, nature of friction, lubricants and lubrication theory, and surface damage and fatigue. Computational analyses of surfaces and lubricant flow are performed using Python.

title: Intermediate Fluid Mechanics
description: Introduction to vectors and tensors, deformation and stress in fluids, kinematics of fluid flows, conservation laws, Navier-Stokes equations, energy equation, introduction to computational fluid dynamics (CFD), introduction to vorticity dynamics and selected topics in compressible fluid flow.

title: Intermediate Heat Transfer
description: Study of conduction, convection, and radiation. Steady and transient one - and multi-dimensional heat transfer with emphasis on analytical methods, numerical techniques, and approximate solutions.

title: Inviscid Flows
description: Introduction to the dynamics of inviscid, incompressible fluids; vector representation theorems; vorticity transport theorem; solution methods to steady and unsteady, two-dimensional, axisymetric and three-dimensional flows; computational methods for inviscid flows; and forces and moments on bodies in two-dimensional flows.

title: Computational Fluid Dynamics
description: Study of numerical methods tailored to solve thermo-fluids governing equations. Classification of partial differential equation (PDE). Finite difference method.  Basic concepts of discretization, consistency, and stability. Applications of numerical methods to selected model PDE.  Numerical methods for inviscid flow, boundary-layer flow, and the Navier-Stokes equations.  Applications include supersonic compressible and subsonic incompressible flows.  Turbulence modeling.  Finite volume method.  Completion of ME 3321 Fluid Mechanics or equivalent recommended.

title: Viscoelasticity
description: The Theory of Viscoelasticity is fundamental in the study of time rate dependent materials, with specific emphasis on applications to engineering systems with plastics and materials with polymeric behavior.

title: Introduction to Aeronautics
description: Introduces the applied science of atmospheric flight.  The course teaches about airplanes and how they fly from a design and application perspective.  Included are topics in fluid dynamics, airfoil and wing theory, aircraft performance, stability, and aircraft design.

title: Analysis and Design of Propulsion Systems
description: Introduction to compressible flow, including flows with simple area change, heat addition, friction, and shock waves.  Analysis, parametric design, and performance of ramjets, turbojets, turbofans, and turboprops.  Introduction to the operating principles of major engine components.  Introduction to rockets.

title: Wind Energy
description: This course presents fundamentals about wind turbines, both commercial and residential.  Included are topics in aerodynamics, structures, power generation, control economics, environments, noise, and design.

title: Linear Systems
description: Mathematical introduction to time-varying linear systems; state space descriptions, controllability, observability, Lyapunov stability, observer-based control. Design of linear quadratic regulators and infinite-horizon Kalman filters.

title: Intermediate Numerical Methods
description: Topics will include: roots of equations, optimization, linear systems, integration and differentiation, curve-fitting, and systems of ordinary differential equations.

title: Theory of Elasticity
description: The Theory of Elasticity is fundamental to the study of linear and non-linear solid mechanics.  This course introduces the foundations of elasticity for a deformable body, including the concept of deformation and stress using tensor calculus.

title: Continuum Mechanics
description: Introductory course into the mechanics of a continuous medium.  Topics include the foundational concepts of stress, strain, and constitutive relationships presented in Cartesian tensor notation. Studies will focus on both solid and fluid mechanics.

title: Rocket Propulsion
description: Introduction to performance analysis of liquid and solid chemical rockets systems. Efficiency and other derivations, along with Thrust and Flight profile development.

title: Cardiovascular Engineering and Instrumentation
description: See BME 5357 for course information.

title: Renewable Energy Devices
description: Educates graduate students from engineering disciplines in the design and applications of various renewable energy sources, materials, and devices.  Introduces the basic concepts, principles, potentials, and limitations of several energy conversion and storage devices with a particular focus on solar cells, fuel cells, batteries, and supercapacitors.

title: Principles of Process Engineering
description: Overview of the principles of process engineering, where process engineering is broadly described as converting matter from raw materials to more valuable forms. The fundamental engineering principles applied to chemical, environmental, and biomolecular processes.

title: Advanced Concepts of Process Engineering
description: Provides a deeper examination of some process engineering topics initially covered in the “Principles of Process Engineering” course. Topics include reaction engineering, unit operations, chemical measurements, process data analysis, and flowsheeting of a process.

title: Introduction to Additive Manufacturing
description: This course introduces various aspects of additive manufacturing, which has become prominent in industry over the past two decades. The course gives the students a basic understanding of additive manufacturing and its use in design, both for rapid prototyping and for functional manufacturing. The course highlights the advances that additive manufacturing makes upon traditional manufacturing techniques.

title: Properties and Processing of Electronic Materials
description: Study of the design and applications of conventional and advanced electronic materials ranging from typical Si and electroceramics to complex oxides and conducting polymers.  Fundamental issues controlling their properties, processing, and reliability are addressed.  In addition, a variety of thin film deposition techniques such as dc/rf magnetron sputtering, thermal/e-beam evaporation, and chemical vapor deposition are covered.

title: Metal Additive Manufacturing
description: Metal additive manufacturing (AM) printing technologies, devices, capabilities, materials, and applications.  Trade-offs between various metal AM processes and technologies, along with the various software tools, processes, and techniques enabling customization.  Includes hands-on experience encompassing processing-structure-property-performance relations.

title: Welding Metallurgy
description: Structure–property–processing relationships in fusion and solid-state welding. Thermal cycles, phase transformations, and microstructural evolution in ferrous and nonferrous alloys; heat-affected zones; weldability; residual stresses and distortion; defects and discontinuities; testing and qualification standards. Laboratory emphasizes welding procedures, metallographic characterization, and mechanical testing.

title: Microscopy Techniques for Materials Science
description: Introduction to the basic principles of structural, chemical and property characterization techniques. The course is grounded in modern X-ray diffraction and electron microscopy techniques for characterization of the chemical and structural properties of a material. The course introduces the fundamental theoretical framework for diffraction, spectrometry and imaging methods.

title: Advanced Mechanics of Materials
description: Analysis of stresses and strains at a point, stress-strain relationships, stresses due to various loading conditions, and theories of failure. Includes energy methods, shear center, unsymmetrical bending, curved beams, torsion in closed and open cell cross-sections, principles of plastic analysis, and buckling analysis.

title: Mechanical Behavior of Polymers and Polymeric Composites
description: Elastic and viscoelastic behavior of polymers and polymeric composites, predicting long-term behavior from short-term tests using  time-temperature-superposition; relating chemical structure to mechanical properties for thermosets, thermoplastics, and semi-crystalline plastics; relating processing to mechanical properties; and predicting stiffness and strength from properties of fibers and polymeric matrices.

title: Deformation and Fracture in Metals
description: This course introduces students to advanced theories of deformation and fracture that limit lifetimes in service of components and structures made of metals and alloys. Fracture mechanics are introduced as a tool in the life prediction of components that develop cracks before catastrophic failure. Plastic collapse, creep, fatigue, and environmental stress cracking are covered. Failure analysis methodology and tools are introduced and illustrated.

title: Fracture Mechanics
description: Basic elements of fracture mechanics, including elastic and elastic-plastic crack tip stress and strain fields, and fracture toughness. Methods for prediction of crack growth under static and fatigue loading using stress intensity factor, J integral and energy release rate, and their applications to materials assessment.

title: Failure Analysis: Theory and Practice
description: Introduction to basic failure theories and their application to the analysis of component and system failure in service; methodology of systematic failure analysis of actual service failures; introduction to tools used in failure analysis; case studies used extensively for teaching and assignments.

title: Special Topics in Engineering
description: Study of special topics in mechanical engineering. This course may be repeated for a total of six times with different topics.

title: Special Topics in Engineering
description: See EGR 5397 for course information.

title: Internship Experience
description: Provides graduate students opportunity for internship work experience in computer science-related positions with consent of major advisor.

title: Master's Thesis
description: Students completing a master's program with a thesis must complete six hours of ME 5V99 .

title: ME Doctoral Research Colloquium
description: A regularly scheduled, graduate-level seminar featuring speakers from engineering and science programs at Baylor, industry, federal laboratories, medical schools, and other universities. Topics covered may include current research activities, research ethics, research methods, the advisor-advisee relationship, publication preparation, proposal development, presentation construction, and career path investigations.

title: Advanced Topics in Engineeering
description: Study of advanced topics in engineering. This course may be repeated for a total of four times.

title: Engineering Research
description: Doctoral students may enroll in up to 12 semester hours of engineering research hours prior to taking the preliminary exam and being accepted into candidacy for the doctoral degree.  These engineering research hours will count toward the degree.

title: Dissertation
description: Required of all doctoral candidates.  In no case will fewer than 12 semester hours be accepted for a dissertation.  Students may not enroll for doctoral research hours until they have been officially accepted into candidacy for the doctoral degree.  After initial enrollment, students must register for at least one semester hour of doctoral research every term thereafter (summer term excluded).

title: Emergency Airway, Anesthesia, and Pain Management
description: The MEM 6210 rotation orients the emergency medicine physician assistant (EMPA) to the concepts and application of emergency airway management, the spectrum of anesthesia, and pain management in emergency medicine. This is a comprehensive rotation composed of a two-week rotation within the hospital setting.

title: Emergency Treatment of Orthopedic Injuries, Emergency Ultrasounds, and Emergency Radiology
description: Study of concepts of orthopedic conditions encountered in the emergency department.

title: Toxicology and Oral Maxillary Facial Disorders
description: This rotation studies concepts of toxicological presentations and oral maxillary facial disorders encountered in the emergency department.

title: Cardiovascular, Pulmonary, Hematologic, Oncologic, and Psychosocial Diseases and Disorders
description: The study of concepts of cardiovascular, pulmonary, hematologic, onocologic, and psychosocial diseases encountered in an emergency department environment.

title: Gastrointestinal, Genitourinary, Obstetrics, and Gynecology Diseases
description: The study of concepts in gastrointestinal, genitourinary, obstetrics, and gynecology diseases encountered in an emergency department environment.

title: Pediatric Non-Traumatic Musculoskeletal Disorders, Abuse, and Assault
description: The study of diseases, non-traumatic muscular skeletal disorders, assault and abuse in the pediatric emergency department patient.

title: Emergency Department 3
description: General emergency medicine rotation to apply the concepts of medical practice in an emergency department setting.

title: Emergency Department 4
description: General emergency medicine rotation to apply the concepts of medical practice in an emergency department setting.

title: Emergency Department 5
description: General emergency medicine rotation to apply the concepts of medical practice in an emergency department setting.

title: Medical Research Design
description: This course introduces medical research design. This course focuses on generation of a testable research question, developing a research design that addresses the question and conducting a literature review that supports the study design.

title: Approaches to Medical Data Collection and Analysis
description: This course introduces approaches to medical data collection and analysis. This course focuses on executing a research protocol, collecting outcome measures accompanying data analysis.

title: Techniques for Medical Research and Presentation
description: This course introduces various techniques for medical research presentation. This course takes information obtained in developing a high-quality clinical research project, demographic and outcome measure data and statistical analysis and generates oral and written products for doctoral defense and presentation at local and national venues.

title: Concepts of Emergency Medicine 3
description: This didactic course provides foundational knowledge and skills for the physician assistant to practice in the specialty of emergency medicine. It will introduce emergency medicine physician assistants (EMPA) to the concepts of emergency stabilization, treatment, and resuscitation of common conditions and concerns seen in the emergency department. The key areas of focus include emergency resuscitation, endocrinology, environmental injuries, gastroenterology, genitourinary, gynecology, infectious disease, neurology, obstetrics, orthopedics, trauma management, pediatrics, wound management, and social issues. This will form the basis of knowledge for clinical rotations.

title: Introduction to Emergency Medicine
description: A comprehensive orientation to the field of Emergency Medicine, with formal presentations/lectures, ACLS/PALS, Emergency Department administrative issues, Emergency Medical Services, ethics and professionalism, and an introduction to research in emergency medicine.

title: Clinical Emergency Medicine 1
description: This clinical course introduces emergency medicine physician assistants (EMPA) to the concepts of emergency stabilization, treatment, and resuscitation of common conditions and concerns seen in the emergency department. The key areas of focus include obtaining patient histories, performing physical examinations, developing differential diagnoses, procedural competency, and management of emergency department patients. This course facilitates the translation of medical knowledge into clinical application.

title: Clinical Emergency Medicine 2
description: This clinical course introduces emergency medicine physician assistants (EMPA) to the concepts of emergency stabilization, treatment, and resuscitation of common conditions and concerns seen in the emergency department. The key areas of focus include obtaining patient histories, performing physical examinations, developing differential diagnoses, procedural competency, and management of emergency department patients. This course facilitates the translation of medical knowledge into clinical application.

title: Clinical Emergency Medicine 3
description: This clinical course introduces emergency medicine physician assistants (EMPA) to the concepts of emergency stabilization, treatment, and resuscitation of common conditions and concerns seen in the emergency department. The key areas of focus include obtaining patient histories, performing physical examinations, developing differential diagnoses, procedural competency, and management of emergency department patients. This course facilitates the translation of medical knowledge into clinical application.

title: Clinical Emergency Medicine 4
description: This clinical course introduces emergency medicine physician assistants (EMPA) to the concepts of emergency stabilization, treatment, and resuscitation of common conditions and concerns seen in the emergency department. The key areas of focus include obtaining patient histories, performing physical examinations, developing differential diagnoses, procedural competency, and management of emergency department patients. This course facilitates the translation of medical knowledge into clinical application.

title: Clinical Emergency Medicine 5
description: This clinical course introduces emergency medicine physician assistants (EMPA) to the concepts of emergency stabilization, treatment, and resuscitation of common conditions and concerns seen in the emergency department. The key areas of focus include obtaining patient histories, performing physical examinations, developing differential diagnoses, procedural competency, and management of emergency department patients. This course facilitates the translation of medical knowledge into clinical application.

title: Emergency Department 6
description: This clinical course introduces emergency medicine physician assistants (EMPA) to the concepts of emergency stabilization, treatment, and resuscitation of common conditions and concerns seen in the emergency department. The key areas of focus include obtaining patient histories, performing physical examinations, developing differential diagnoses, procedural competency, and management of emergency department patients. This course facilitates the translation of medical knowledge into clinical application.

title: Clinical Research
description: The MEM 6346 rotation consists of a didactic phase during the first month of training, dedicated research blocks, and individual research days scheduled throughout the 18-month course. The research course is designed to familiarize the residents with the research process and, more importantly, to facilitate the development of the skills necessary to critically analyze published scientific articles.

title: Concepts of Emergency Medicine 1
description: This didactic course provides foundational knowledge and skills for the physician assistant to practice in the specialty of emergency medicine. It will introduce emergency medicine physician assistants (EMPA) to the concepts of emergency stabilization, treatment, and resuscitation of common conditions and concerns seen in the emergency department. The key areas of focus include anesthesia, diagnostic imaging, endocrinology, gastroenterology, genitourinary, gerontology, gynecology, hematology, neurology, obstetrics, oncology, ophthalmology, orthopedics, otolaryngology, toxicology, pain management, pediatrics, psychiatry, and social issues. This will form the basis of knowledge for clinical rotations.

title: Concepts of Emergency Medicine 2
description: This didactic course provides foundational knowledge and skills for the physician assistant to practice in the specialty of emergency medicine. It will introduce emergency medicine physician assistants (EMPA) to the concepts of emergency stabilization, treatment, and resuscitation of common conditions and concerns seen in the emergency department. The key areas of focus include cardiology, diagnostic imaging, emergency resuscitation, endocrinology, environmental injuries, hematology, infectious disease, neurology, oncology, orthopedics, oral maxillofacial conditions, trauma management, toxicology, psychiatry, pulmonology, wound management, and social issues. This will form the basis of knowledge for clinical rotations.

title: Pediatrics Emergency Department
description: A rotation emphasizing the clinical side of pediatric patients in the emergency department.

title: Pediatrics Emergency Department and Pediatric Intensive Care Unit
description: A rotation emphasizing the clinical side of pediatric patients in the Emergency Department and Pediatric ICU.

title: Emergency Ultrasound
description: The MEM6445 rotation orients the emergency medicine physician assistant (EMPA) to the concepts and application of ultrasound in emergency medicine. This is a comprehensive rotation composed of a four-week rotation of ultrasound (US) in the Emergency Department.

title: Medical Intensive Care Unit (MICU)
description: A rotation emphasizing life threatening diseases encountered in the emergency department and managed in the MICU.

title: Cardiac Care Unit (CCU)
description: A rotation emphasizing the concepts of cardiovascular diseases encountered in the emergency department and managed in the CCU.

title: Investments
description: Through a study of portfolio theory and asset-pricing models, students acquire the analytical skills necessary to conduct valuations of equities, fixed income securities, and alternative investments. This course applies theoretical concepts to investment analysis and portfolio management.

title: Orientation to General Surgery
description: This rotation emphasizes the clinical skills of providing care and treatment  to patients with surgical disease.

title: Operations Strategy: Concepts and Fundamentals
description: This module introduces a framework for defining a company's operating system and evaluating its operations strategy, and provides an overview of key diagnostic and analytical tools for identifying, framing, and solving strategic operating issues.

title: Operations Strategy: Structuring the Operating System
description: This module covers key tools for resolving the challenges of operational networks, setting capacity levels and allocating capacity within the network, and establishing a strategy for operational improvement, and examines the key issues that a firm faces in establishing its operations strategy.

title: Operations Strategy: Managing Operational Focus
description: This module provides frameworks for decisions on how firms should approach the execution of fundamental changes in their operating systems and addresses how various processes and systems are designed and managed in a way that builds superior and rapidly improving performance. Particular attention is placed on ways to balance the competing objectives of operational focus and growth.

title: Global Human Capital Leadership
description: Participants learn to manage people and lead organizations to gain competitive advantage through human capital. Objectives include understanding, analyzing, and implementing human resource management practices through collaboration across functional areas, and apply human resource principles to improve global organizational performance.

title: Negotiations: Power and Influence
description: This course focuses on the structure of the negotiation and brings in the impact of power, influence, and politics in organizations. Students will participate in class discussions, simulations, and role play, as well as being exposed to the latest research in the area of negotiation.

title: Strategic Planning
description: Discovery of how businesses and corporations develop their strategic plan using a framework for how companies approach customers, competitors, and employees. Throughout this course, students will seek to identify issues and problems facing companies in the development of their plans in domestic and international arenas. In addition, the various components of a strategic plan will be studied by using examples of companies that succeeded or failed.

title: Strategy Implementation
description: Insight into putting the strategic plan into action. Students will build upon the ideas discussed in MGT 5186 and will assess the effectiveness of the strategy implementation in companies recognized in domestic and international markets. In contrast, companies that have not implemented their strategies will also be assessed.

title: Strategic Control
description: Development of an understanding pertaining to companies competence in maintaining high performance, and their adaptation to the dynamics of their industries.

title: Leading Organizational Change for High Performance
description: This course is intended to help managers and leaders better understand and diagnose behavior in organizations. They can apply this information in an ethical manner to influence positive organizational change.

title: Negotiations: Maximizing Multi-Party Outcomes
description: Enhances individual effectiveness in the workplace and marketplace through the development of negotiating skills and advanced understanding of negotiation when there are more than two parties. Emphasis is on practical application of theory through a variety of skill-building exercises. Topics include distributive and integrative bargaining tactics, leverage, framing, and cognitive biases, within a multi-party setting, and team negotiations.

title: In Residence: Global Strategy: Building & Sustaining Competitive Advantage
description: During this in-residence experience students engage with global organizations and leaders to expand their depth of knowledge related to all aspects of strategic management. Culture, leadership, operations, strategy, societal impact, and their intersections are explored as students build their critical-thinking skills and consider the challenges faced by executives of global enterprises.

title: Management of Organizational Behavior
description: Management of Organizational Behavior enhances students’ knowledge regarding behavioral science concepts relevant to the study of organizational and managerial behavior.  The design of the course is active learning through developing skills as a manager, role play, and an extensive hands-on organizational analysis project with local organizations.  Topics examined include, but are not limited to, leadership, motivation, teams, talent development, individual differences, global issues, ethics, and organizational change.  The framework used is one of organizational development as students are prepared to manage human capital effectively.

title: Leading with Integrity
description: This course is intended to help managers and leaders better understand the theories of leadership by utilizing leadership development tools, models of ethical decision making, and organizationally-relevant applied projects.

title: Operations and Supply Chain Management
description: This course examines the principles and practices of Operations and Supply Chain Management (OSCM) that enable organizations to effectively produce and deliver goods and services. Students learn to analyze and improve operations through topics such as demand planning, sourcing, inventory management, production and distribution, quality management, Lean and Six Sigma principles, and decision theory. Emphasis is placed on the integration of operations, marketing, finance, and supply chain functions in creating world-class performance. Experiential projects, simulations, and live case studies develop analytical and problem-solving skills. Stewardship-oriented OSCM practices are highlighted as essential for sustainable competitive advantage, ethical leadership, and organizational excellence.

title: Leadership in the Global Marketplace
description: Examination of cultural differences, their impact on business practices, and how to successfully adapt global business practices to different cultures. Study of strategic global expansion and the key role of leadership in effective globalization. This course engages students with a trip to study business in a host country and engage in a business challenge.

title: Management Decision Models
description: Application of analytical models and computer simulation to managerial problems in various functional areas. Topics examined include mathematical programming, network analysis, decision theory, waiting line validation, and implementation of computer simulation models.

title: Project Management
description: This course covers issues important in effective project management.  It considers project planning, budgeting, evaluation, and auditing. It also examines methods for monitoring projects, analyzing risk, and allocating resources. [This course also prepares students for the Certified Associate in Project Management and Project Management Professional certification exams.]

title: Advanced Project Management: The Systematic Implementation of Complex Organizational Project
description: Complex project management requires high-performance project managers capable of dealing with the chaos of today’s organizational environment. This seminar focuses on the advanced project-management skills, systems thinking, and process modelling needed to implement complex organizational programs and manage projects for business process improvement.

title: Seminar in Human Resource Management
description: Subjects discussed are: changing equal employment opportunity laws and case rulings, recruitment, selection methods, total compensation systems, performance evaluation, and organizational justice. Emphasis throughout is on practical application of the theory for organizational effectiveness.

title: Management of Employee Relations
description: Analysis of union-management relations in both private and public sectors. Subjects include negotiation techniques and strategies, discipline and discharge, discrimination, sexual harassment, labor contract interpretation, EAP programs, safety, management rights, seniority systems, working conditions, and others. Role playing, negotiations simulation, and analysis of arbitration cases are used. Research paper required.

title: Negotiation and Conflict Resolution
description: Enhances individual effectiveness in the workplace and marketplace through the development of negotiating skills and advanced understanding of negotiation and persuasion. Emphasis is on practical application of theory through a variety of skill-building exercises. Topics include distributive and integrative bargaining tactics, team and multiparty negotiations, leverage, framing, and cognitive biases.

title: Global Supply Chain Strategy
description: Course provides students with key concepts and strategies for coordination of suppliers, factories, warehouses, distribution centers, and retail outlets to produce and distribute items to the right customers, at the right time, and at the right price to minimize costs while satisfying a certain target service level. Strategic management decisions include the linkages among demand planning, global sourcing, and distribution channel management.

title: Supply Chain Analytics
description: This course focuses on the application of supply chain analytics (SCA) on key supply chain processes and activities, including supply management, warehouse and inventory management, demand management, and logistics management. Students will learn techniques and applications used in SCA to gain insights that contributed to effective decision-making in the supply chain. In addition, the course draws will explore emerging technologies that affect SCA.

title: Organizational Design and Development
description: Fundamentals of designing/redesigning an organization. Major issues include: designing individual jobs and subunits, handling interdependencies among jobs and subunits through coordination and control techniques, dealing with resistance to change, and promoting flexibility. Creating/maintaining a high level of organizational effectiveness is the overarching theme. Students interested in general management, management consulting, and positions in organization development departments would benefit in particular from the course.

title: Management Consulting
description: This course is designed for individuals interested in business and management consulting. It uses live consulting projects with local businesses that require the application of skills taught in a master’s program. It also emphasizes soft skills utilized in management consulting such as teamwork, customer relationship management, and change management. Other topics include resolving critical conflicts and utilizing strategic frameworks.

title: Negotiation
description: This class enhances critical thinking skills, particularly in the context of group interactions and negotiation.  It focuses on understanding the theory and practice of negotiation in a variety of settings.  Students learn to develop skills experientially and analogically and to understand negotiation in useful analytical frameworks.

title: Global Strategy: Building and Sustaining Competitive Advantage
description: Global Strategy: Building and Sustaining Competitive Advantage provides the opportunity to extend the work completed in previous courses for the purpose of analyzing the problems and issues encountered by executives of the global enterprise.

title: Managing For Higher Performance
description: This course teaches students to connect organizational behavior theory with current management practice to implement improved management skills in current and future careers.

title: Operations Management
description: This course develops skills in describing and understanding operating processes and measuring and analyzing those processes, and the ability to develop and evaluate plans for positively changing those operating processes within the context of the entire organization and in harmony with the firm's strategic mission.

title: Global Supply Chain Strategy
description: Course provides students with key concepts and strategies for coordination of suppliers, factories, warehouses, distribution centers, and retail outlets to produce and distribute items to the right customers, at the right time, and at the right price to minimize costs while satisfying a certain target service level. Strategic management decisions include the linkages among demand planning, global sourcing, and distribution channel management.

title: Strategic Management and Business Policy
description: This course provides students with an opportunity to understand strategic management in organizations in a variety of industries by studying competition, resources, capabilities, innovation, alliances, mergers, acquisitions, and company structures.

title: Integrative Executive Decision Making
description: Integration of operational analysis with other functional areas. Computer models simulate the effects of various strategies on manufacturing plants, information flow environments, and distribution systems. The first half of the course focuses on individual skill development for use in the second half analyzing and solving core problems within the student's company.

title: Special Studies in Management
description: This course may be taken for one to six semester hours of credit.

title: Doctoral Seminar in Organizational Behavior
description: This course takes a holistic view to understand how the behaviors, attitudes, and emotions of individuals affect and are affected by the organizational context.  Psychological theories of human behavior are reviewed in order to examine the mechanisms driving human behavior within organizational contexts at the individual, group, and organizational levels.

title: International Business
description: The objective of this course is to familiarize students with the environment in which international companies operate. Students will be introduced to the special problems and complexities of operating in the global marketplace, addressing issues in the fields of accounting, economics, finance, law, marketing, organizational behavior, politics, production, and strategy.

title: Customer Analytics
description: This course will enable students to: apply basic skills in data manipulation and visualization using various software packages, analyze customer data with the help of different statistical tools and techniques and use the findings from data analyses to make managerially relevant marketing decisions anchored in Customer Analytics. BBA students must have a declared major to enroll in this course.

title: Seminar in Marketing Administration-Planning
description: Taught from the perspective of a mid-to upper-level marketing manager. Students will develop an understanding of marketing strategy and its role in today's complex business environment. Topics include an overview of the marketing planning process with an emphasis on target market selection and marketing plan development and the strategic aspects of marketing management.

title: Seminar in Marketing Administration-Implementing
description: Presentation of the strategies and tactics involved in a marketing program from the perspective of a mid-to upper-level marketing manager. The course content assumes a basic understanding of marketing principles while using lectures, readings, and case analyses. Product, pricing, promotion and distribution issues will be discussed with an emphasis on the interrelationships between marketing decisions. Marketing ethics and social responsibility and their importance in marketing decision-making will also be discussed.

title: Seminar in Marketing Administration-Adapting
description: This seminar will be taught from the perspective of a mid-to upper-level marketing manager using lectures, readings, and case analyses presenting a variety of topics involving the adaptation of current marketing practices in differing situations. Topics will include interfunctional relationships, international marketing, total quality management, and the assessment of marketing expenditures.

title: Decision Based Marketing
description: This course will confront the challenge of designing and implementing a successful combination of marketing variables to make informed decisions about the firm's strategy in its target markets. The course also addresses the importance of companies being market-driven and customer-focused as well as presenting current marketing management practices.  As decision makers, the students will learn to implement analytic perspectives, decision models, and marketing concepts to assist with decisions involving communications strategies, product offering, pricing, and distribution channels.

title: Seminar in Marketing Strategy
description: Role of marketing decision making in achieving corporate objectives; planning and implementing the marketing program; product research and development, distribution problems, promotional strategies, and pricing analysis. Attention will be given to the new marketing application of quantitative methods and the behavioral sciences.

title: Seminar in Comparative Marketing
description: Marketing structures, functions, and institutions in different national environments. Emphasis is placed on the manner in which different economic systems condition and shape the nature of marketing.

title: Seminar in Marketing Research
description: First-hand experience with real-life marketing research problems, including such areas as research design, sampling, experimental design, parametric and nonparametric data analysis techniques, and computer statistical programs.

title: Marketing Communications
description: Statistical techniques and their applicability to business decision making. Topical coverage includes multiple regression, analysis of variance, factor analysis, discriminant analysis, cluster analysis, and multidimensional scaling.

title: Business to Business Marketing
description: Marketing by firms to organizations rather than to households. Negotiation strategies are emphasized along with management of relationships, purchasing, distribution channels, and distribution logistics.

title: Strategic Brand Management
description: This marketing course will provide participants the opportunity to develop a better understanding of branding strategy and its role in today's complex business environment. This course will cover the leading theories, models, and other tools to make better branding decisions, how to plan and evaluate branding strategies, and provide a forum for students to apply these principles.

title: Theory and Practice in Customer Relationship
description: The understanding of systems, dependencies, variability, and interrelationships--including the ability to manage systems--is an essential element in customer relationship management (CRM). Therefore, the organization and the supply chain as interrelated systems is the starting point for this course. From that foundation, students will move into assessing measurements, the tools for analyzing an organization's current business processes and flows, and the means for integrating these into customer management initiatives.
